channel.title: MIKE Research Clusters
channel.description: Recent content on MIKE Research Clusters

item.title: Associate Professor Dr. Suppawong Tuarob
item.description: Biography Dr. Suppawong Tuarob is an Associate Professor and the Head of the Machine Intelligence and Knowledge Engineering Research Clusters (MIKE). Recently recognized among the Top 100 Researchers, his work fundamentally targets the intersection of multi-disciplinary AI applications… Education Ph.D. in Computer Science and Engineering, The Pennsylvania State University M.S. in Science, Industrial Engineering, The Pennsylvania State University B.S. in Computer Science and Engineering, University of Michigan

item.title: MIKE Lab Secures Multiple Acceptances at NeurIPS 2026
item.description: We are thrilled to announce that the MIKE Research Clusters will be well-represented at NeurIPS 2026 in Vancouver. Three of our primary research tracks—Precision Health, Social Intelligence, and Systemic Discovery—each secured a spot in the main track. These papers highlight our commitment to engineering AI solutions that are not only theoretically sound but also ready for real-world deployment. Congratulations to the student leads and faculty mentors involved in these high-impact projects.

item.title: Strategic Research Retreat: Khao Yai 2026
item.description: The MIKE Lab recently concluded its Annual Strategic Research Retreat at Khao Yai. Moving beyond the screen, the team engaged in intensive whiteboard sessions focused on our 2027 research roadmap, specifically targeting frontiers in autonomous space exploration and personalized healthcare. Beyond the science, the retreat served as a vital team-building exercise, featuring board game marathons and nature treks, reinforcing the multidisciplinary bond that defines our research clusters.

item.title: Automated Acne Severity Grading via Label Distribution Learning
item.description: Summary of Contribution In this paper, we introduce a robust training protocol that utilizes cross-dataset normalization to mitigate the high variance in human-expert labeling. Our results demonstrate a 14% improvement in mean squared error compared to traditional CNN-based grading systems.

item.title: MIKE Lab Awarded International Grant for Autonomous Satellite Analysis
item.description: We are pleased to announce a new partnership with the Space Research Foundation. This grant will fund the development of low-latency, on-board AI architectures capable of identifying Near-Earth Objects directly from satellite hardware, reducing the data bottleneck between space and ground stations.

item.title: Precision Health Pillar Initiates Multi-Site Clinical Validation
item.description: Bridging the gap from lab to clinic, our diagnostic AI is now being tested by practicing dermatologists. This phase of research focuses on model interpretability and the reduction of inter-observer variability in high-pressure clinical environments.

item.title: Version 2.0 Release: Temporal Knowledge Graph (TeReKG) Toolkit
item.description: The MIKE Lab is committed to open science. Version 2.0 of our TeReKG toolkit provides enhanced support for linking GitHub repositories to academic citations, helping software teams maintain architectural consistency over long-term development cycles.— title: “Version 2.0 Release: Temporal Knowledge Graph (TeReKG) Toolkit” date: 2026-02-15 draft: false image: “/images/news/software-release.jpg” teaser: “The lab releases an updated open-source framework for linking scholarly code to technical documentation using contrastive learning.” venue: “Open Source Initiative” categories: [“Software”] tags: [“Systemic Discovery”, “Software Engineering”, “TeReKG”] The MIKE Lab is committed to open science.

item.title: AI & Equity: MIKE Lab Contributes to Regional Policy Brief
item.description: Our work on abusive language detection in low-resource languages has led to a collaborative policy brief. We are providing computational insights to ensure that digital social environments remain safe and inclusive as AI integration accelerates across the region.

item.title: Contrastive Learning for Near-Earth Object Detection in Deep Space Exposures
item.description: Summary of Contribution By applying temporal knowledge graphs (TeReKG) to link sequential exposures, our architecture successfully identified three previously uncatalogued near-Earth objects during our validation phase at the regional observatory.
